pages.full_text:
We walked on to the City of London Museum in the Barbican. There was a temporary exhibition of bones from London. We saw a magnificent coach. The Fire of London with Pepys' voice. A Victorian Shop. I could spend longer there. We had lunch + set of for the Tate.
	At the Tate was a Sargent exhibition. R.L. Stevenson was there from the Taft Museum. Gorgeous portraits. A real treat.
	· Then we returned back to the campsite + home for the winter.
pages.notes: Text runs across the gutter; right-hand portions of lines are visible only in the R image. 'set of for' likely reads 'set off for' — transcribed as written. 'Taft Museum' is as written; likely refers to the Taft Museum, Cincinnati (lender of R.L. Stevenson portrait to the Sargent exhibition). No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
[Continuation of left-page text across gutter — see A_0262_L for full_text.]

Photo caption (written vertically beside glued-in photo): John Singer Sargent  The Misses Vickers 1884 (Sheffield)
photo_captions: John Singer Sargent  The Misses Vickers 1884 (Sheffield)
pages.notes: Right page contains the right-hand portions of all text lines from the left page (gutter split), plus a glued-in photograph of the painting 'The Misses Vickers' (1884) by John Singer Sargent, with a handwritten caption written vertically to the left of the photo. No handwritten page number visible in top-right corner.
